ORDER ON DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE LATERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)
MINUTES FOR JUL-24-2018 9:30 AM

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE CITY FAILED TO SATISFY ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN SHOWING: 1) THE INAPPLICABILITY OF THE FAILURE TO WARN EXCEPTION TO THE HAZARDOUS RECREATIONAL ACTIVITY IMMUNITY; 2) THE CITY'S PLACEMENT OF THE LIGHT POLE AND SURROUNDING FENCE DID NOT INCREASE THE RISKS INHERENT IN PLAYING BASEBALL; AND 3) THE LIGHT POLE AND SURROUNDING FENCE IS NOT A DANGEROUS CONDITION. EVEN IF THE CITY HAD SATISFIED ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN ON THESE ISSUES, THERE ARE TRIABLE DISPUTES AS TO EACH OF THEM. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Off Calendar

Defendant San Francisco Latin American Baseball Leagues Notice Of Demurrer To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO LATIN AMERICAN BASEBALL LEAGUE'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted: Defendant San Francisco Latin American Baseball League's demurrer to the second and third cause of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Kai Kopp is overruled as to the second cause of action for negligence and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for negligent supervision, hiring and training. The negligence cause of action, but not the negligent supervision cause of action, is based on the same general set of facts as the complaint. Both the complaint and second cause of action in second amended complaint allege negligence with regard to the location of the light pole. The third cause of action in the second amended complaint is based on alleged negligence with regard to the supervision and conduct of the umpires which is nowhere mentioned in the complaint. Since the third cause of action does not relate back to the filing of the complaint, it is time-barred. Counsel for the League is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/HEK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Continued To May-13-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment. (Part 3 Of 3 Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment is a duplicate entry.

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment. (Part 2 Of 3 Of Tentative Ruling.)
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment is a duplicate entry.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Edward M. Higginbotham of the Bryant Law Group, tel. No. 1-415-581-0885, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Tim Fama, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. No. 1-415-554-3921 appearing for the
Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied. The City failed to satisfy its summary judgment burden showing: 1) the inapplicability of the failure to warn exception to the hazardous recreational activity immunity; 2) the City's placement of the light pole and surrounding fence did not increase the risks inherent in playing baseball; and 3) the light pole and surrounding fence is not a dangerous condition. Even if the City had satisfied its summary judgment burden on these issues, there are triable disputes as to each of them. (See word minutes for details). Order Signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-18-2018 Continued To Jul-12-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion To Amend The Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Edward Higginbotham, Esq. 415-581-0885 for Plaintiff Kai Kopp appears late, after the case has been called.
Hunter Sims III, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the Cty Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Defendant informs the Court parties have agreed to a different Trial Date. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAI KOPP Motion To Amend The Complaint.

Plaintiff Kai Kopp's motion to file his proposed second amended complaint is granted on the conditions that: a) the filing of the amended complaint does not affect the City's pending motion for summary judgment which will remain on calendar as noticed and b) the trial date is continued to May 13, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 and the only discovery that is permitted after the current discovery cutoff date is discovery propounded by defendant Latin American Baseball League to the new legal and factual allegations alleged by Mr. Kopp in his second amended complaint. If Mr. Kopp agrees to both conditions in writing or orally at the hearing, Mr. Kopp has leave to file his second amended complaint no later than June 26, 2018. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any time. The two conditions eliminate any possible legal prejudice to the defendants resulting from the granting of the motion.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
